llm_result.summary: The case describes migraine unpredictability driving anticipatory anxiety, plan-avoidant behavior, and a perceived feedback loop where anxiety may worsen migraines. The following modifiable BIO-PSYCHO-SOCIAL levers are designed to reduce (a) timing uncertainty, (b) functional impact, (c) avoidance of commitments, (d) anticipatory anxiety, (e) beliefs that anxiety worsens migraines, and (f) low migraine self-efficacy. Interventions span biological regulation (sleep, activity, hydration, nutrition), cognitive-behavioral and mindfulness-based strategies, social engagement, and structured self-management education. Each lever is a changeable predictor with simple, monitored metrics over an 8–12 week horizon, enabling a feedback-informed, multi-domain plan while avoiding pharmacological dosing instructions.

solutions.predictor_variable: Migraine timing forecasting with daily diary + probabilistic planning
solutions.solution_candidate: Implement a structured daily migraine diary that tracks onset timing, duration, intensity, and possible triggers, plus a probabilistic forecast for the next 24–48 hours to guide planning and commitments.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: By collecting objective timing data and offering a forecast mechanism, the person gains information about likely onset windows, reducing unpredictability, improving confidence in planning, and decreasing anticipatory anxiety; this also enhances perceived control and self-efficacy.
solutions.measurement: - Diary completion rate (days/week >= 5). - Forecast accuracy: proportion of days where predicted window captures actual onset within +/- 12 hours. - Planning commitment rate (planned activities kept per week). - Pre-planning anxiety rating (0–10) before major plans.
solutions.mapping_query: Query used to map migraine timing/forecast data to C01, C03, C04, C06: high-variability timing mapped to planning and anxiety-related targets.

solutions.predictor_variable: CBT-based anxiety coping for migraine-related anticipatory anxiety
solutions.solution_candidate: Deliver a brief CBT-based program (either self-guided workbook or therapist-assisted) focusing on anticipatory anxiety, cognitive restructuring of worry about attacks, exposure to feared activities with safety planning, and skills to increase migraine self-efficacy.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reducing catastrophic thinking and anxiety about onset can lower anticipatory distress, break the feedback loop between anxiety and migraines, and increase confidence in managing episodes.
solutions.measurement: - State anxiety before activities (0–10) weekly. - Migraine days per week (self-report). - Migraine management self-efficacy score (0–100) weekly.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns unless elevated distress requires clinical escalation.
solutions.mapping_query: CBT-based coping mapped to C04 (anticipatory anxiety), C05 (anxiety-migraine link), C06 (self-efficacy).

solutions.predictor_variable: Sleep regularity and circadian stability program
solutions.solution_candidate: Establish a consistent sleep-wake schedule, optimize sleep environment, and limit caffeine/alcohol in evenings; combine with a brief psychoeducation component about sleep-migraine links.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Regular sleep reduces migraine vulnerability and stabilizes mood/anxiety; improved sleep can lessen unpredictability and anticipatory distress, with downstream functional gains.
solutions.measurement: - Sleep duration variance per week (SD). - Bedtime/wake time consistency (percentage of days within set window). - Weekly migraine days and functional impairment (0–10 scale).
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for sleep disorders; consider clinician input if persistently poor sleep.
solutions.mapping_query: Sleep regulation targets C01 (unpredictability) and C04 (anticipatory anxiety) and C02 (functional impact).

solutions.predictor_variable: Mindfulness-based stress reduction (MBSR) / ACT to tolerate uncertainty
solutions.solution_candidate: Introduce weekly mindfulness or ACT-based practices to increase nonjudgmental awareness of uncertainty, reduce reactivity to symptoms, and shift toward value-driven goals with less avoidance.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Mindfulness/ACT builds tolerance to uncertainty and reduces affective amplification of symptoms, weakening the anxiety-migraine feedback loop and improving self-efficacy.
solutions.measurement: - Practice adherence (minutes per week). - State anxiety before plans (0–10). - Migraine-related functional impairment (0–10) weekly.
solutions.mapping_query: Mindfulness/ACT mapped to C04 (anticipatory anxiety), C05 (anxiety-exacerbation belief), C06 (self-efficacy).

solutions.predictor_variable: Structured social engagement plan to reduce avoidance
solutions.solution_candidate: Create a monthly calendar of low-barrier social activities with contingency planning (backup plans) to increase commitment and reduce plan-avoidance due to migraine uncertainty.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Proactive scheduling and social exposure reduce avoidance, maintain life participation, and improve daily functioning despite migraine variability.
solutions.measurement: - Planned activities kept per week (yes/no). - Weekly WSAS or functional impact score. - Migraine days during social weeks.
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure activities are adaptable to migraine flares; avoid overcommitment.
solutions.mapping_query: Social engagement targets C03 (avoidance) and C02 (functional impact).

solutions.solution_candidate: Implement a gradual, physician-approved aerobic exercise program (e.g., brisk walking 150 minutes/week) with self-monitoring of exertion and migraine diary.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Exercise improves vascular regulation, sleep, mood, and stress resilience, potentially reducing migraine unpredictability and functional impairment.
solutions.measurement: - Weekly exercise minutes; adherence rate. - Weekly migraine days. - Sleep quality (0–10) and perceived fatigue.
solutions.safety_notes: Screen for cardiovascular risk; tailor intensity; stop if headaches worsen.
solutions.mapping_query: Aerobic exercise mapped to C01 (unpredictability) and C02 (functional impact).

solutions.solution_candidate: Introduce a daily hydration target and regular meals to minimize dehydration-related migraine risk and mood/energy fluctuations.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Stable hydration/nutrition reduces physiological migraine triggers and supports mood stability, potentially reducing unpredictability and impairment.
solutions.measurement: - Daily water intake (ml) with adherence percentage. - Meal regularity adherence (percent of meals on schedule). - Weekly migraine days and functional impact.
solutions.mapping_query: Hydration/nutrition plan mapped to C01 and C02.

solutions.predictor_variable: Migraine self-management education & contingency planning toolkit
solutions.solution_candidate: Provide an education pack covering triggers, self-monitoring, contingency planning for migraines, and skills for returning to activities quickly post-attack.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved knowledge and contingency planning increase perceived control, reduce uncertainty, and facilitate rapid re-engagement after attacks, mitigating functional impact and anxiety.
solutions.measurement: - Knowledge assessment score (0–100). - Contingency plan use rate (yes/no). - Time to resume activities after a migraine (hours). - Self-efficacy for migraine management (0–100).
solutions.mapping_query: Education/contingency planning mapped to C01 (unpredictability), C02 (functional impact), C04 (anticipatory anxiety), C06 (self-efficacy).
